steps.title: Write inches per foot
steps.content:
The scale says $1$ model inch represents $\frac{19}{4}$ actual feet. So $1$ actual foot corresponds to
$$
\frac{1}{19/4}=\frac{4}{19}
$$
model inches.

steps.title: Multiply by actual length
steps.content: For $x$ actual feet, multiply by the number of model inches per actual foot: $$ x\cdot \frac{4}{19}=\frac{4x}{19} $$ The answer is C.
